01 — Featured Work
Curated final cuts across promos, recaps, and motion identity work. Built for brand clarity, audience retention, and polished delivery.
SHOWREEL · 2024 — Portfolio promo — motion, editing, and rhythm across brand and narrative work.

EVENT · 2024 — Leadership program recap — interviews, b-roll, and clear narrative arc.

03 — Live Production
Live environments demand precision, speed, and calm execution under pressure. From local house-of-worship streams to multi-country regional broadcasts, delivery stays broadcast-ready.
PRODUCTION LEAD
Led weekly multi-camera livestream operations — camera switching, graphics overlay, audio mixing, and real-time troubleshooting for consistent broadcast quality.

• Multi-camera direction and switching
  • Real-time graphics and lower-thirds
  • Audio mixing and broadcast monitoring
  • Team coordination under live pressure
RECORDING & STREAMING COORDINATOR
Coordinated recording and live streaming for a global faith-based outfit across East Africa — technical setup, camera coverage, and reliable delivery to distributed online audiences.

• Multi-day regional event coverage
  • Live stream technical direction
  • Post-event content packaging
  • Cross-functional crew leadership
04 — Signal path

Newest first: Bachelor of Public Relations at Daystar (from May 2025), then WebMaster at Healthy U 2000 (through Oct 2025). Moringa School (DevOps and full-stack certifications, 2023–2024) sits before the creative-internship stack: Kenyans.co.ke → CTN → Philmax, with the Eikonisma contract running alongside Daystar and those placements. The diploma years (2020–2022) close the reel.
SPINE · 25·05 → …
May 2025 — present
Continuing formal study in public relations — building on a communications diploma and years of design, broadcast, and digital newsroom practice.

• Pursuing a Bachelor of Public Relations; programme began May 2025.
  • Extends prior Daystar diploma work (Communications & Media, Electronic Media) with a PR-focused degree path.
SPINE · 25·01 → 25·10
January 2025 — October 2025
Nairobi on-site: end-to-end web, integrations, and mobile — from AI-assisted support to POS-linked loyalty and store operations tooling.

• Built an AI agent/chatbot with LangGraph, LangChain, OpenAI API, Qdrant, MongoDB, and Render; sole developer alongside a data analyst — ~25% faster support responses.
  • Integrated AI APIs with iVend (POS) for loyalty data and personalized promotions; custom APIs and system wiring — ~15% lift in customer retention.
  • Shipped cross-platform staff/customer apps with React Native and Firebase; ~20% higher engagement.
  • Dashboards in React.js and D3.js for inventory and ops; ~15% efficiency gain on tracking workflows.
  • WordPress on Siteground tuned for performance (~30% faster loads); internal tools with cross-functional teams (~10% workflow gains).
SPINE · 23·08 → 24·09
August 2023 — September 2024
DevOps first, then full-stack — ops discipline and shipping practice before end-to-end application development, both in Nairobi.

• DevOps Engineering (Aug–Nov 2023).
  • Full-Stack Software Engineering (Jan–Sept 2024).
  • In-person programmes at Moringa School, Nairobi.
LAYER · 22·04 → 22·07
April 2022 — July 2022
National digital desk pace: viral edits, live pressers, and campaign visuals where news breaks in hours, not days.

• Viral news video editing; shoots and edits for social formats.
  • TeamKenya and campaign concepts; photography and retouch for story packages.
  • Live-streaming pressers and interviews; event coverage (e.g. TSO, Waterfront Karen).
  • Brainstorming for client campaigns (including Diageo); in-house interviews with public figures.
LAYER · 21·11 → 22·03
November 2021 — March 2022
Broadcast rhythm: lineups, overlays, and social artwork in a television production environment.

• Digital materials, image retouching, and edits for TV program lineups.
  • Motion graphics templates and overlays for shows and social platforms.
  • Voice-overs for adverts and promos; daily social creative.
LAYER · 20·11 → 21·04
November 2020 — April 2021
First deep dive into transit and OOH while enrolled at Daystar: PSV, campus branding, and pitch-ready mockups at real-world scale.

• Print and digital materials; sizing artwork for ubers, buses, billboards, and digital screens.
  • Research on high-traffic routes; coordination with advertising infrastructure partners.
  • Mockups for client pitches; collaboration with Creative Director and Design Manager.
SPINE · 19·11 → 22·12
November 2019 — 2022

Contract engagement structured so Daystar coursework could continue alongside steady communications and design delivery — and so internships (Philmax, CTN, Kenyans) could layer on without leaving the role.

• Communication strategy and workplace training (CRM, conflict resolution, brand management).
  • Graphic content, illustrations, and infographics from conception through delivery.
  • Brand consistency across marketing work; liaison between marketing and design for deadlines.
  • Contract format made concurrent study and internship blocks workable across the same period.
SPINE · 20·01 → 22·12
2020 — 2022
Diploma years overlapping the Eikonisma contract and the Philmax → CTN → Kenyans internship arc — formal media theory alongside paid practice.

• Communications and media studies with major in electronic media.
  • Completed alongside contract work and sequential internships; foundation before the 2025 Public Relations degree.

More than an editor or designer alone: a hybrid builder who combines creative execution, technical systems, and strategic communication for stronger campaign outcomes.
End-to-end content creation — from concept to final delivery across video, motion, design, and photography.
